meaning: n.涤罪,炼狱,暂时受苦的地方
examples: Each cake eaten would represent a soul being freed from Purgatory.
Although it’s highly unlikely your results well be positive, those two weeks are like purgatory.
As night fell and All souls' Day arrived "bells were rung for the souls in purgatory" writes Rogers.
France, Germany, Japan--we would join you in economic purgatory but for the saving grace of startups.
Passing the flapjacks with gregarious strangers at a bed-and-breakfast would be like starting the day in purgatory for me.
